content:
Hi @Philip Durbin 🚀
We have the date!
May 26-29 2026

(May26th: optional pre-conference day)
image.png

Felipe Gómez-Cortés said:

Hi Philip Durbin 🚀
We have the date!
May 26-29 2026

Hi those who collaborate with some issues can assist too? If don't have anything in my calendar for those days I would like going there, for spending the nights I won't have much problem as I have family in Terrassa
3 messages were moved here from #community > #Dataverse2025 by Philip Durbin 🚀.
Thanks for the announcement, @Felipe Gómez-Cortés! Do you already know the address of the venue where the meeting will be?

Hi @Philipp Conzett!
We have not an address yet.

Hi everyone,

We’re happy to share that the Dataverse Community Meeting 2026 will be held in Barcelona, 26–29 May 2026, hosted by BSC-CNS and Harvard University. :tada:

We’re now looking for sponsors and partners interested in supporting this key event for the global Dataverse and open science communities.

If your organisation might be interested, please reach out to us at dcm2026@bsc.es. We’ll be sharing a detailed sponsorship proposal soon.

:raised_hands: Thanks for spreading the word!

The DCM2026 Organising Committee

Hello, long time no see friends. We have a quick question regarding custom metadata blocks. These can be enabled for datasets, can they also be enabled for collections (sub dataverse metadata)? Our use case is we want to mark which collections/sub dataverses can have their metadata exported and shown as collections in other online repositories. Thank you, Asbjørn
DCM2026.jpg

Philip Durbin 🚀 said:

DCM2026.jpg

Has there been a date change?

Philip Durbin 🚀 said:

DCM2026.jpg

My last exam on university it's on 23 May, if it's in 12-15 f i would not be able to assist, so will have to wait to further editions

Rachel Fernandez said:

Has there been a date change?

Oh! I didn't even notice! Apparently, yes!

oh no, if the event has been moved by two weeks to earlier, i might not be able to attend anymore :sweat_smile: is there any way to sign up for important announcements already, or is this thread the best place? i tend to book travel quite in advance, so would be good to keep up with any changes :sunglasses:
is 12-15 May the confirmed final dates? :thinking:

Hi there!
Yes, there was a change.
12-15 May is the confirmed date.

Dear Dataverse Community,
We’re excited to share some updates about the Dataverse Community Meeting 2026 (DCM2026)!
The event will now take place on 12–15 May 2026 at the Barcelona World Trade Center, right by the sea in the heart of Barcelona.
We look forward to seeing you there!

Best regards,
The DCM2026 Organising Committee
The call for papers is open! https://dcm2026.com/call-for-papers/
I announced it in the Jupyter Zulip.
awesome! it'd be great to have an idea when the call for submissions closes!
When you click through it says, "The deadline for abstract submission is 29 January 2026, at 23:59 CET."
ah, thank you! didn't think to click through :joy:
There's a list of key dates at https://dcm2026.com/general-information/

Hi Dataverse Community!,

Just a friendly reminder after the holiday break that the Call for Papers for the Dataverse Community Meeting 2026 (Barcelona) is open.

🗓 Submission deadline: 29 January 2026
:link: Call for Papers: https://dcm2026.com/call-for-papers/
:link: Conference themes: https://dcm2026.com/conference-themes/

We hope this reminder is helpful for those who may have missed the original announcement during the end-of-year period.

We look forward to your submissions and to welcoming you in Barcelona.

Best,
The DCM2026 Organising Committee
Heya @Felipe Gómez-Cortés. Just a heads up that I reposted this call to the Code 4 Lib mailing list.

Great!
Thanks @Julian Gautier !!
@Felipe Gómez-Cortés will working groups have a chance to meet?
@Sonia Barbosa is on the organizing committee and said she'd report back on in-person meetings for working groups.

Hi @Philip Durbin 🚀

To keep things consistent with the 2025 format at UNC, we have indeed reserved Tuesday, May 12th specifically for Satellite Meetings.

We’re excited to host these at the Barcelona Supercomputing Center (BSC) installations! The main conference will then take place at the World Trade Center (WTC) from May 13th–15th.

You can see the location details here: https://dcm2026.com/general-information/. We’ll be sharing more specifics soon on how groups can coordinate their space at the BSC!

And speaking about the schedule...

:siren: ONLY 2 DAYS LEFT TO SUBMIT! :siren:

If you want to present a talk or poster during the main sessions at the World Trade Center, the deadline is fast approaching:

  • Deadline: 29 January 2026, 23:59 CET

  • Theme: AI, Interoperability, and Sensitive Data

  • Submit here: https://dcm2026.com/call-for-papers/

@Felipe Gómez-Cortés so I talked to the three working groups I'm part of (Documentation, pyDataverse, and Containerization) and we'd each love to take you up on your offer of having a 1-hour working group meeting on Tuesday, May 12th at BSC. Since some of us (like me) are part of multiple working groups, it would be ideal if the groups don't meet at the same time.

@Felipe Gómez-Cortés @Sonia Barbosa is there a reason why I can't submit my last two proposals and only save them as drafts?
image.png

@Oliver Bertuch I suppose it is for this reason:

  • Each Presenting author can submit a maximum of three (3) abstracts.
    https://barcelo.eventsair.com/PresentationPortal/dcm2026/abstractsportal/Presentation
Drat! I didn't see that!
Thanks @Juan !
In filling out proposals.... is Affiliation meant to be the university I work for, and then Organization for the department I work in? Or do I have that reversed? Thanks!
I don't know who can update it, but I just noticed DCM26 isn't on the events overview page of the dataverse.org website: https://dataverse.org/events

Hi everyone!

We’ve heard from several community members who need just a bit more time to polish their proposals. To make sure everyone has a chance to share their work, we are extending the deadline!

:spiral_calendar: New Deadline: Monday, February 2, 2026, at 23:59 CET

Whether you are focusing on AI, Interoperability, or Sensitive Data, you now have the weekend to finalize your abstracts for the main conference in Barcelona.

:point_right: Submit here: https://dcm2026.com/call-for-papers/

We can’t wait to see your submissions! :rocket::bar_chart:

Dieuwertje Bloemen said:

I don't know who can update it, but I just noticed DCM26 isn't on the events overview page of the dataverse.org website: https://dataverse.org/events

Good point! Fixed!

Felipe Gómez-Cortés disse:

Hi everyone!

We’ve heard from several community members who need just a bit more time to polish their proposals. To make sure everyone has a chance to share their work, we are extending the deadline!

:spiral_calendar: New Deadline: Monday, February 2, 2026, at 23:59 CET

Whether you are focusing on AI, Interoperability, or Sensitive Data, you now have the weekend to finalize your abstracts for the main conference in Barcelona.

:point_right: Submit here: https://dcm2026.com/call-for-papers/

We can’t wait to see your submissions! :rocket::bar_chart:

Good to know. Slight extension but quite useful.

Hi everyone!

We’ve heard your requests for more time, so we are extending the submission window with two separate deadlines:

  • :speaking_head: Talks: Monday, February 2 (23:59 Pacific Time)

  • :picture: Posters: Monday, February 16 (23:59 Pacific Time)

Showcase your work! :bar_chart: For the posters, we especially encourage the community to show off their datasets, collections, or specific Dataverse installations. It’s a great way to share how you are using the platform in the real world.

:point_right: Submit your abstracts here: https://dcm2026.com/call-for-papers/

Take advantage of the extra time to polish those proposals. We can't wait to see what you're working on! :rocket:
I already have too many proposals, but I really like the idea for installation posters!

Hi Community!
We have some exciting updates:

  1. Talks Update: The call for talks was a massive success! We received over 100 abstracts—thank you for such an incredible response. :confetti:

  2. Posters (Deadline Feb 16!): You still have time to join the program! We want to see your Dataverses, Collections, and real-world success stories. Don't miss this chance to showcase your work. :point_right: Submit here: https://dcm2026.com/call-for-papers/

  3. Registration: Early Bird rates are available until February 28. Secure your spot at the best price! :point_right: Register here: https://dcm2026.com/registration/

See you in Barcelona! :flag_spain:
@Felipe Gómez-Cortés will it be possible for my company to pay the registration fee via bank transfer?

Hi @Balázs Pataki !!
Yes, no problem!
If you would like to pay by bank transfer, please contact us at: dcm2026@bcocongresos.com.

Hi @Felipe Gómez-Cortés After a working group leader has submitted their registration for the conference and included a list of people who will attend their working group's meeting on May 12, will other people who aren't on that list but who have registered for the conference be able to attend that working group meeting?
Let me ask María Ferrer and I will come back to you @Julian Gautier

@Julian Gautier Here's the answer:
According to BSC rules, the main contact must provide the full names and email addresses of all attendees by April 30th. Only guests on the official meeting guest list will be admitted.

Therefore, it's best to have the guest list with a few extra names to process your access cards, rather than trying to process your access at the last minute.
Ah that's clear. Thanks! I'll let the working group leads know.
Hmm, it sounds like working group leads should start gathering a list of attendees pretty soon, if the list must be submitted at registration, which is already open.
@Felipe Gómez-Cortés thanks for the info!
Oh, I imagined that if leads wanted to add more names before April 30 but after they already registered, they could just email you Felipe or a colleague. Right?
Yes @Julian Gautier, we can append new attendees to the list in new mails before April 30th.

Hi @Felipe Gómez-Cortés Would you happen to know already at what time the programme will end on Friday? (We are looking at the flight schedules, and wonder if we would miss a lot taking an earlier flight back.)
Hi @Laura Huis in 't Veld, we don't have Friday's schedule yet. We will share the program once it is defined.

Hi everyone!

Two big reasons to head over to the Registration Page this week:

  1. Early Bird Ending: You have until February 28th to secure the reduced rate. Don't leave it for the last minute!

  2. May 12th is Filling Up: Our Satellite Meetings (at the BSC) are taking shape! We have already confirmed sessions for:

    • Workshops: Kubernetes (K8s) and others.

    • Working Groups: Containerization, PyDataverse, and Documentation.

    • GDCC Meetings: The Steering Committee Meeting.

  3. Program Reveal: We are putting the final touches on the schedule and expect to release the official program later this week! :calendar:

Want to host your own? The registration form is the primary mechanism to propose a new satellite meeting or joint brainstorm session. If you want to gather a group on May 12th, let us know through the form so we can coordinate the space!

See you in Barcelona! :rocket:

Hola @Felipe Gómez-Cortés! I am planning on registering soon, but wanted to know if the registration will include any meals? I need to include this information in my institutional travel request. Thanks!

Felipe Gómez-Cortés said:

  1. May 12th is Filling Up: Our Satellite Meetings (at the BSC) are taking shape! We have already confirmed sessions for:

    • Workshops: Kubernetes (K8s) and others.

If you are interested in this years edition of the Kubernetes workshop (no plain Docker session this time, so we have more time for big stuff!), let us know in #containers > K8s Workshop DCM26

Last year the room was packed, so please don't destroy our (my) illusions about people being eager to learn about modern tech stacks!
@Oliver Bertuch Thanks for the heads-up! We have people at DataverseNO who are interested. Please allows us a couple of days to figure out how many we'll be. Thanks!
@Oliver Bertuch Could you share this information also in the Dataverse Communinty Google group? I think there is broad interest in this!
Orly? Sure, can do.
@Oliver Bertuch your post looks great! Thanks for spreading the word!

Rachel Fernandez ha dicho:

Hola Felipe Gómez-Cortés! I am planning on registering soon, but wanted to know if the registration will include any meals? I need to include this information in my institutional travel request. Thanks!

Hi @Rachel Fernandez!
The registration includes breakfast and lunch for the main conference.

Laura Huis in 't Veld ha dicho:

Hi Felipe Gómez-Cortés Would you happen to know already at what time the programme will end on Friday? (We are looking at the flight schedules, and wonder if we would miss a lot taking an earlier flight back.)

I can answer now!

Hi everyone!

We have some very important updates regarding DCM 2026 in Barcelona:

1). Early Bird Extended!
Good news! We have extended the Early Bird registration deadline. You now have until March 9th (23:59 CET) to secure the discounted rate.
:point_right: Register here: https://dcm2026.com/registration/

2). Preliminary Program is LIVE!
The moment you’ve been waiting for! The preliminary program is now available on our website. Check out the incredible lineup of talks, keynotes, and sessions.
:point_right: View the Preliminary Program: https://dcm2026.com/preliminary-programme/

3). Action Required: Confirm Your Participation! ⚠
The committee has sent out the final resolutions for Posters.

  • If you received an acceptance email: Please check your inbox and confirm your attendance immediately.
  • We still have many pending confirmations, and we need yours to finalize the official schedule!
  • If you were accepted for a Poster, please keep an eye out for the notification and reply as soon as possible.

We can't wait to see the community come together in Barcelona! :rocket:

Hi everyone,

This is a final reminder that the Early Bird registration for DCM 2026 closes this coming Monday, March 9th, at 23:59 CET.

If you haven't secured your spot yet, make sure to register this weekend to take advantage of the reduced rates.

:point_right: Register here: https://dcm2026.com/registration/

We are looking forward to seeing the community in Barcelona! :castle:

Hi everyone!

Two major updates as we start the week:

:one: Keynote Speakers Confirmed! The full lineup of keynote speakers for DCM 2026 is now live. We are thrilled to host experts like Ian Foster (Argonne), Hilary Hanahoe (RDA), Stefaan Verhulst (GovLab), and more. Check out their full bios and session abstracts: :point_right: Meet the Keynotes: https://dcm2026.com/keynote-speakers/

:two: Early Bird Deadline: TODAY Today, March 9th, is the final day to register at the reduced rate. The discount expires tonight at 23:59 CET. Don't miss out on the best price! :point_right: Register Now: https://dcm2026.com/registration/

See you in Barcelona! :castle:

I was able to get my registration in by the deadline and look forward to seeing everyone there!

Now to getting all my travel setup :airplane_arrival:

Any one have recommendations on which hotels to stay at? Recommended areas? :thinking:
Yay! Glad you're coming! I haven't clicked it yet, but there's a "book your accommodations" link at https://dcm2026.com/accommodation/

After a very very very extensive search for the best room at an affordable price (OMG so many options!!!), I finally can say: I will stay in a nice AirBnB quite close to the venue. There are some others available at the same place, maybe go grab that now :smile:

Oliver Bertuch said:

After a very very very extensive search for the best room at an affordable price (OMG so many options!!!), I finally can say: I will stay in a nice AirBnB quite close to the venue. There are some others available at the same place, maybe go grab that now :smile:

There are so many options, but glad you found something! I think even just knowing what area would be good to stay at might be helpful.

I had a PM exchange a while ago and got these recommendations:

I would suggest to be in a less touristic area, like Sants which is a less expensive area. It is very easy to reach the venue by metro L3 (green line).
https://maps.app.goo.gl/cnrEpvj1Bw8ZSvm96

Or even better, something a the neighbourhood Gracia.
The L3 metro station is Fontana, Lesseps or Diagonal
https://maps.app.goo.gl/jssXBcbP2MEnNnWc9

As I will be staying for a few days after the conference, I decided to go with something much closer to the venue. I can take the V11 bus at the front door of the venue or just walk. My Air BnB is within this area: https://maps.app.goo.gl/5gfqomGXAtx1nnEb6

So sight seeing after the conference is all doable in walking distances and I don't loose a lot of time commuting in and out of the city centre.
I've also noticed that everyone and their cat is sharing the smallest broom closet as a room... It was hard to pick something that at least has a window to get some air!
@Oliver Bertuch thank you for sharing this. beware I will only call the V11 the "Beyoncé Bus".

@Felipe Gómez-Cortés when registering, there is no prefix country code for the United States (or I can't find it!):

It's ok, though. I'll just put +1 before my number, I guess.

Hi Community!

Following up on our planning for the satellite sessions:

So far, we have officially registered the Documentation WG meeting. We now need the organizers for Containerization and pyDataverse to send over their proposals and, crucially, the list of in-person attendees. The Sensitive Data and Large Data groups are also very welcome to submit their proposals as soon as possible.

Regarding the logistics at the BSC:

  • Hybrid Setup: All meeting rooms at the BSC are equipped for hybrid sessions, so hosting remote participants is no problem.

  • Organizer Responsibility: It is the responsibility of each WG lead to bring their own laptop and any necessary adapters (screens generally use HDMI). You will also need to create and manage your own Zoom/meeting links for the virtual portion.

  • Security & Attendance: Important: We specifically need a separate, accurate list of in-person attendees for security and access control at the BSC. Please ensure this list does not include those attending only online.

Looking forward to getting these finalized!

@Felipe Gómez-Cortés I have a proposal for the Large Data Support working group and am in the process of gathering a list of attendees. When I have that, should I send that info to you, or update my registration for the satellite meeting?
@Felipe Gómez-Cortés I just registered and included a satellite meeting for the Containerization Working Group.

Deirdre Kirmis ha dicho:

Felipe Gómez-Cortés I have a proposal for the Large Data Support working group and am in the process of gathering a list of attendees. When I have that, should I send that info to you, or update my registration for the satellite meeting?

Hi @Deirdre Kirmis , you can submit the proposal with an initial list of attendees and update this list by the end of April. It is great that the Large Data Support WG will meet :grinning_face_with_smiling_eyes:

Philip Durbin 🚀 ha dicho:

Felipe Gómez-Cortés I just registered and included a satellite meeting for the Containerization Working Group.

Great @Philip Durbin 🚀 ! Thanks for the thread. I am going to update the logistic team about these two satellite meetings.

Apologies if I missed this, but I would like to attend a pre-conference session or two, but I don't know what they are or how to sign up for them. If it's available, could someone share a schedule?

I was just reading above and perhaps it's all still being planned. Will there be a general call for participation once all set? I just don't want to miss out. :)

@Bethany Seeger for working groups, at least, I would suggest reaching out in these places to say that you'd like to attend:

  • #large-data > DCM2026 working group meetings
  • #docs > docs wg at DCM 2026 in Barcelona
  • #python > pyDataverse wg at DCM 2026 in Barcelona
  • #containers > Containerization wg at DCM 2026 in Barcelona

Each group is responsible for giving an updated attendee list before the conference, by April 30th, I believe.
As for other satellite meetings, I don't believe there's a way to sign up yet. I assume it's coming.

Hello Community:
I am looking for community members who work as data curators/repository managers to serve on a curation panel during the community meeting.

Please contact me if you plan to attend the community meeting so we can discuss -

Thanks
Sonia

Hi everyone!

We are officially approaching the conference week, and the program is now finalized! :rocket:

Please help us spread the word through your own communities. You can now explore the full lineup here:

  • Full Programme: https://dcm2026.com/preliminary-programme/
  • Keynote Speakers: https://dcm2026.com/keynote-speakers/

:warning: Standard Registration Reminder:
Don't miss the deadline! Make sure to register by next Wednesday, April 15th to secure your spot.

  • Register here: https://dcm2026.com/registration/

We can't wait to see you all in Barcelona next month!

I see everyone here booked early and prepared more extensively than me : ) I'm both glad for all those involved, and saddened by my decreased options now. Anyway. Asking the important questions - is the soccer match happening, and if so, Thursday or Friday?

Thomas van Erven said:

I see everyone here booked early and prepared more extensively than me : ) I'm both glad for all those involved, and saddened by my decreased options now. Anyway. Asking the important questions - is the soccer match happening, and if so, Thursday or Friday?

(Hotel arranged, still arguing with flights a bit but it appears no matter what I do, it'll be a doozy of a flight schedule anyhow)

Hi @Thomas van Erven !!!
Don't worry about being "late" to the party—the important thing is that you're coming!

Regarding the important questions: Yes! The soccer match is being scheduled for Tuesday, May 12th.
We really hope to see you on the pitch! :football::stadium:

Safe travels with those flights!

Felipe Gómez-Cortés said:

Hi Thomas van Erven !!!
Don't worry about being "late" to the party—the important thing is that you're coming!

Regarding the important questions: Yes! The soccer match is being scheduled for Tuesday, May 12th.
We really hope to see you on the pitch! :football::stadium:

Tuesday is perfect, see you then : ) That means I'll be able to make my flight on Friday as well, which is in the words of Borat, "very nice".

Hi everyone!

A little reminder: today, April 15, is the last day for standard registration for DCM 2026.

If you haven't registered yet, please do so before 23:59 CET tonight to help us finalize the catering and logistics with the BSC and WTC venues.

:point_right: Register here: https://dcm2026.com/registration/

We’ve got a fantastic program lined up and we can't wait to see the whole community together in Barcelona next month! :castle:

Anyone willing to bet if there will be enough kerosine available so we all can travel to Barcelona by mid May? Don't wanna jinx it, but a slight furrowed brow appears... Let's root for ourselves we can see each other!
I'll bet you a beer in Barcelona. And the very same worry crossed my mind
Sounds like I could only loose with that bet - either I have to buy you a beer or we're not going :sad: "Gamble responsibly" :victory:
Oh surely I'd be buying the beer. I think we'll be largely fine - shortages expected end of May (so holiday season is in peril, but not the community meeting).

Hi everyone!

Two important updates as we head into the final days of April:
1. Final Registration Deadline: April 30th :time_ticking: This is the absolute last call for late registrations! If you or your colleagues haven't signed up yet, please do so by Thursday to ensure we have you in the system for the BSC and WTC venues. :point_right: Register here: https://dcm2026.com/registration/
2. Featured Satellite Meetings :rocket: We have two fantastic proposals that dive deep into specialized topics. I’ve attached the full PDFs for you to check out:

  • "Can the Physical World Make AI more Trustworthy?" by Ben Lewis (Focus on Space, Time, and Metadata).

  • "From Data to Publication and Back" by Łukasz Opioła (Focus on connecting active research data workflows with repo-publication).

Action needed: If you are interested in participating in either of these, please contact the organizers directly (emails are in the PDFs) by May 3rd. We need to finalize the in-person access credentials for the BSC by May 5th, so please don't delay!
DCM2026_DataToPublication_LukaszOpiola.pdf
DCM2026_SpatioTemporalMetadata_BenLewis.pdf.pdf

Hope to see you soon in Barcelona!

DCM2026 Logistics Commitee

Related: the following in-person working group sessions on Tuesday are also still open for participants. All you need is to give them your name by April 30th. You can reach out to them via zulip:

  • #large-data > DCM2026 working group meetings
  • #docs > docs wg at DCM 2026 in Barcelona
  • #python > pyDataverse wg at DCM 2026 in Barcelona
  • #containers > Containerization wg at DCM 2026 in Barcelona

Hi everyone! Today is a big day for DCM 2026 logistics. Please take note of the following:

1. Registration Closes Today! :time_ticking: If you haven't registered for the main conference in Barcelona, today (April 30th) is the absolute deadline. :point_right: Register now: https://dcm2026.com/registration/
2. Satellite Meeting Participation (May 12th) Today is also the cutoff to coordinate with organizers for the pre-conference sessions. Reach out via these streams/emails:

  • #large-data > DCM2026 working group meetings

  • #docs > docs wg at DCM 2026 in Barcelona

  • #python > pyDataverse wg at DCM 2026 in Barcelona

  • #containers > Containerization wg at DCM 2026

  • Data To Publication: Łukasz Opioła (lukasz.opiola [at] cyfronet.pl)

  • AI Trustworthiness/Metadata: Ben Lewis (bglewis [at] gmail.com)

3. Preliminary Schedule :calendar: Here is the draft schedule for May 12th. It remains subject to change until all participant lists are finalized.

4. :warning: Note for Organizers: Please send your final participant lists to the Logistics Committee ASAP. We need these to finalize the schedule and process security access badges for the BSC!

Hope to see you soon in Barcelona!
DCM2026 Logistics Committee
What is the GDCC WG Data Curation that is mentioned in the program? Is that a closed event? I haven't seen anything about that.

Hi everyone! We are opening one final round of promotion for our May 12th Satellite Meetings at the BSC.

If you wish to attend any of these sessions, you must contact the organizers by Thursday night (CET) to be included on the security access list. Places are limited!
Workshops (Please email organizers):

  • AI & Metadata: Ben Lewis — bglewis [at] gmail [dot] com

  • Born Managed: Rory MacNeil — rmacneil [at] researchspace [dot] com

  • Data to Publication: Łukasz Opioła — lukasz.opiola [at] cyfronet [dot] pl

  • GDCC Sustainable Path: Philipp Durbin — philip_durbin [at] harvard [dot] edu

  • K8s Workshop: Oliver Bertuch — o.bertuch [at] fz-juelich [dot] de (Note: This is separate from the Containerization WG)

Working Groups (Join the threads below):

  • #large-data > DCM2026 meetings

  • #docs > docs wg in Barcelona

  • #python > pyDataverse wg

  • #containers > Containerization wg

[Insert Tentative Schedule Image Here]

Check the attached documents for full abstracts!

Falco (Copenhagen University Library) said:

What is the GDCC WG Data Curation that is mentioned in the program? Is that a closed event? I haven't seen anything about that.

It's a good question. I saw it on earlier, tentative versions of the satellite (May 12th) schedule but perhaps it was never finalized.

Falco (Copenhagen University Library) dijo:

What is the GDCC WG Data Curation that is mentioned in the program? Is that a closed event? I haven't seen anything about that.

Hi @Falco (Copenhagen University Library) and @Philip Durbin 🚀 !

To clarify, the "GDCC WG Data Curation" entry:

  • We originally keep a placeholder open for a potential Data Curation Workshop (gray color).
  • It was mistakenly labeled as "GDCC Working Group" on the previous schedule draft.

A Gastronomic Guide for DCM2026-BCN.

Rule # 1: Do not eat Paella @ La Rambla. It is a Tourist Trap.
Rule # 2: Do not eat @ La Rambla. Seriously, Tourist Trap.
Rule # 3: Tips in Spain are optional, not mandatory.
Rule # 4: We eat late!* Lunch is at 14:00, and dinner starts at 20:30.

https://maps.app.goo.gl/pUqQzSWu5ebTvRWw5

(*Actually, we eat around Solar Noon.)
